[ t TJ. 11015/6/92-^, A TJFT, (188)] 4, %, >siMltfl, # 3 ^ Vfo* -- 7 of 24 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PARTIIâ€”Stc. 3(i)] MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 19th December, 1997 G.S-R. 707(E).â€” ( E ) . â€” In exercise of the powers conferred by section 87 of the Punjab Re-organisation Act, 1966 (31 of 1966), the Central Government hereby extends to the Union territory of Chandigarh, the Punjab Agricultural Credit Operations and Miscellaneous Provisions (Banks) Act, 1978 (Punjab* Act No.5 of 1979), as in force in the State of Punjab on the date of this notification, subject to the following modifications, namely:- MODIFICATIONS In the Punjab Agricultural Credit Operations and Miscellaneous Provisions (Banks) Act, 1978 (Punjab Act No.5 of 1979), â€” (1) in section 1, for sub-sections (2) and (3), the following sub-sections 3hall be substituted, namely:- "(2) It extends to the whole of the Union territory of Chandigarh. (3) It shall come into force at once."; (2) for the worda "State Government", wherever they occur, the word "Administrator" shall be -- 8 of 24 -- [qpTiiâ€”-a^3(Q] Â»irai'vrTPm:3raiwoT substituted and there shall also be made such consequential changes as the rules of grammar may require; (3) for the words "this Act", wherever they occur, except in sub-section (lj of section 1, the words "this Act as extended to the Union territory of Chandigarh" shall be substituted; (4) after the words and figures "the Punjab Co- operative Societies Act, 1961" wherever they occur, the words, "as in force in the Union territory of Chandigarh," shall be inserted; (5) in section 2, clause (a) shall be relettered as clause (aa) and before clause (aaj as so relettered, the following clause shall be inserted, namely:- "(a) "Administrator" means the Administrator of the Union territory of Chandigarh appointed by the Presiderft under article 239 of the Constitution"; (6) in section 3, in sub-section (5) and in sub- section (1) of section 8, for the words "an official of the state Government", the words "an official of the Chandigarh Administration" shall respectively be substitutedj (7) in section 23, sub-section (3) shall be omitted. 3??6/&ÂŁ/r/â€” J2 -- 9 of 24 -- 10 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART IIâ€”SEC. 3(i)] ANNEXURE THE PUNJAB AGRICULTURAL CREDIT OPERATIONS AND MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS (BANKS) ACT, 1978 PUNJAB ACT No. 5 of 1979 [Received the assent of the President of India on the 7th March, 1979, and was first publised for general Information In the Punjab Government Gazette (Extraordinary), dated tho 27th March, 1979.1 AN ACT to make provisions to facilitate adequate flow of credit for agricultural production and development through banks and other institutional credit agencies and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. BE it enacted by the Legislature of the State of Punjab in the Twenty-ninth Year of the Republic of India as follows:- CHAPTER I Preliminary 1. (1) ThlB Act may be called the Punjab Agricultural Short Credit Operations and Miscellaneous Provisions (Banks) Act, 1970. title extent and commence- ment . (2) It extends to ihe whole of the Union territory of Chandigarh. (3) It shall come into force at once. 2. In this Act, as extended to the Union territory of -- 10 of 24 -- - ^ r ^ 3 ( j > ] w?r wr turn: sfwutwi Chandigarh, unless the context otherwise requires,-- (a) "Administrator" means the Administrator of the Union territory of Chandigarh, appointed by the President under article 239 of the Constitution; laa) "agriculture" and "agricultural purpose" shall include making land fit for cultivation, cultivation of land, improvement of land including development of sources of irrigation, raising, protecting and harvesting of crops, horticulture, forestry, planting and farming, cattle breeding, animal husbandry, dairy farming, seed farming, pisciculture, apiculture, sericulture, piggery, poultry farming and such other activities as are generally carried on by agriculturists, dairy farmers, cattle breeders, poultry farmers and other categories of persons engaged in similar activities including marketing of agricultural products, their storage, and transport and the acquisition of implements and machinery In connection with any such activity; (b) "agriculturist" means a person who is engaged in agriculture; fc) "Agro-Industries Corporation" means a company or other body corporate, one of the principal objectives of which is to undertake activities connected with or Intended for thB development of agriculture and not less than fifty-one par centum of the paid up share capital of which is held by the Central Government or by any State Government or Governments or oartly by the Central Government and partly by one or more State Governments; (dj "bank" oaona - ft) a banking company as definod in the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 ; -- 11 of 24 -- 12 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART IIâ€”SEC. 3(i)] (li) the State B a n k of India constituted under the S t a t e Bank of India A c t , 1955 ; (til) a subsidiary bank aB defined in the State B a n k of India (Subsidiary B a n k s ) Act 1959 ; any of the bankH mentioned in column 2 of the First S c h e d u l e to the Banking C o m p a n i e s (Acquisition and Transfer of U n d e r t a k i n g s ) A c t , 1970 ; any banking Institution notified by the Central Government under section 51 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 ; the Agricultural Refinance Corporation constituted under the Agricultural Refinance Corporation Act, 1963 ! (vii) the Agro-Industries Corporation as defined in clause (c) ; (vlil)the Agricultural Finance Corporation Limited, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 1956 ; and (ix) any other financial institution notified by the Administrator in the Official Gazette as a bank for the purposes of this Act as extended to the Union territory of Chandigarh; (e) "co-operative Bociety" means a co-operative society as defined in clause (c) of section 2 of the Punjab Co-operative Societies Act, 1961, the object of which is to provide financial assistance as defined in clause (f) of this section to its members and Includes the co-operative land mortgage bank ; (iv) (v) (vl) -- 12 of 24 -- [Vm IIâ€”TSB5 3 ( i ) ] HRW *PTTF>m : 3ffTHJKU| 13 (f) "financial assistance" means assistance granted by way of loans, advances, guarantee or otherwise for agricultural purpose; (g) "Registrar" means the Registrar as defined in clause (j) of section 2 of the Punjab Co-operative Societies Act, 1961. CHAPTER II RIGHTS OF AGRICULTURISTS TO CREATE CHARGE ON LAND AND INTERESTS THEREIN IN FAVOUR OF BANKS 3 l (1> J t shall be lawful for an agriculturist ton*. Ltiarge on create a charge on the moveable property owned by him orcrnp and other on the crops raised by him, standing or otherwise. t o m n v e a b l e ,, ' "-"property in the extent of his Interest therein, In favour of a bank, favour of to secure financial assistance from that bank, b a n k - notwithstanding that he may not bo the owner of the land on and from which the crop is raised. (2) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the Punjab Co-operative Societies Act, 1961, as in force in the Union territory of Chandigarh or any other law for the time being in force, no charge in respect of financial aslstanco extended by a co- operative society to an agriculturist shall have priority over a charge on the crops raised by him, standing or otherwise, or any other moveable property in respect of any financial assistance given to him by a bank : Provided thai the financial assistance made by the bank is Â«â€˘- (a) prior In point of time to that of the financial assistance extended by the co-perative eociBty; and (b) on a certificate, in the form prescribed, from the -- 13 of 24 -- ' THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART IIâ€”SEC. 3(i)] co-operative society or c o - o p e r a t i v e s o c i e t i e s in the area where the agriculturist r e s i d e s . (3) A co-operative society in the area whore the agriculturist holde land shall on demand by him isBue a certificate referred to in sub-section (2). (4) The Registrar or any other person appointed by him in this behalf shall prepare a list of co-operative societies registered under the Punjab Co-operative Societies Act 1961, as in force in the Union terrllory of Chandigarh and publish the same in the Official Gazete after such interval as may be prescribed. (5) A bank may distrain and sell through an official of the Chandigarh Administration designated In this behalf by the Administrator, the crop or other produce or other moveables charged, to that bank to the extent of the agriculturist's interest therein and appropriate the proceeds of such sale towards all moneys due to the bank from that agriculturist. 4. (1) Where an agriculturist creates a charge on land, or any other immoveable property which he owns or c h a r g e ^ 0 in which he has an interest in respect of any financial l a n d i n assistance given to h i m by a bank, he m a y m a k e a b a n k ^ y " d e c l a r a t i o n on the lines of the form set out in the d e c l a r a t i o n , Schedule or as near thereto as circumstances p e r m i t , declaring that thereby h e c r e a t e s , in favour of the bank, a charge on such land or other immoveable property or h i e interest therein, as the case m a y b e , to secure the financial a s s i s t a n c e given \o him by the bank. (2) A d e c l a r a t i o n m a d e under sub-section (1) m a y be varied from time to time by the agriculturist w i t h the consent of the bank in whose favour the d e c l a r a t i o n has been made and every such variation shall take effect as if it had been an original d e c l a r a t i o n . -- 14 of 24 -- [vmn-Ts^5-3(0] 1TOT1*iT'tNI4i) : â€˘^â€˘HIUI4.U| 15 CHAPTER III CHARGES AND MORTGAGES IN FAVOUR OF BANKS AND THEIR PRIORITIES 5. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the Punjab Co-operative Societies Act, 1961 as in force in the Union territory of Chandigarh, or any other law for the time being in force and notwithstanding that any land or interest therein stands already charged or mortgaged to a co-operative society, it shall bo lawful for an agriculturist to create a charge or mortgage on such land or interest therein in favour of a bank as security for any financial assistance given to the agriculturist by that bank. Removal of d1sabli1ty Jn creation of charges and mortgages 6. (1) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in any law for the time being in force but subject to any prior c)aim of the Administrator in respect of land revenue,-- (a) no charge or mortgage created on any land or Interest therein after the commencement of this Act as extended to the Union territory of Chandigarh in favour of the Administrator or a co- operative society shall have priority over a charge or mortgage on such land or interest created by an agriculturist in favour of a bank as security for financial assistance given to the agriculturist by the bank after the commencement of this Act as extended to the Union territory of Chandigarh and prior to the charge or mortgage in favour of the Administrator or the co-operative society; ami Priority of charges and mortgages in favour of Government, bank and co-ooerat1ve society. (b) any charge or mortgage created on any land or interest therein in favour of a bank in resfl&ct of financial assistance given to an agriculturist by -- 15 of 24 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART IIâ€”SEC. 3(i)J that bank shall have priority over any other charge or mortgage that may have been created over such land or interest in favour of any person other than the Administrator, a co-operative society or any other bank, prior to the date on which the charge or mortgage was created in favour of the bank. (2) Where different charges or mortgages over the same land or interest therein have been created by an agriculturist in favour of the Administrator, a co- operative society or a bank or more than one bank, any such charge or mortgage created as security for financial assistance given by the Administrator, co- operative society or the bank or banks by way of term loans for development purposes Bhall have priority over the other charges or mortgages created in favour of the Administrator, co-operative society or any of the banks, provided prior notice of any euch financial assistance by way of term loan for development purposes had been given to the Administrator, co-operative society or bank and the Administrator, co-operative society or bank has concurred in such financial assistance, and where more than one such charge or mortgage is as security for financial assistance given by way of term loan, the charges or mortgages by way of security for term loan for development purposes will rank for priority in accordance with the dates of their crea ti on. Explanation.-- For the purposes of this section "term loan for development purposes'* shall mean financial assistance which would generally lead to improvement of agriculture or building up of assets in agriculture but Bhall not include financial assistance for meeting working capital, expenses for seasonal agricultural operations and marketing of crops.
